question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the toID parsed from cfg.toAccountID to resolve to a non-existent account on the network, causing Execute(client) to succeed (transaction gossiped) but GetReceipt(client) to return INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID, causing submitWithRetry() to log a failure and retry unnecessarily, wasting operator funds on duplicate gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the timer created at line 49 (`time.NewTimer(sleep)`) is not stopped if ctx.Done() fires first (line 51-53), causing a goroutine leak per failed retry attempt, which over time exhausts goroutine resources and degrades the pinger's ability to submit transactions and gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera SDK's Execute(client) to select a node that is partitioned from the rest of the network (via a poisoned network map from a malicious mirror REST endpoint), causing the transaction to be submitted to an isolated node that cannot gossip it to the rest of the network, resulting in the transaction never reaching consensus?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cfg.amountTinybar is read from an environment variable without cryptographic integrity protection, allowing an attacker with local process environment access to set it to math.MinInt64, causing AddHbarTransfer() to produce an overflow in the transfer amount and Execute(client) to fail with an invalid transaction error, preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera network to return TRANSACTION_EXPIRED on every retry attempt by ensuring the system clock of the pinger host is skewed far into the past, causing the transaction's valid start time to be outside the network's acceptance window and preventing gossip on all attempts?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential (non-concurrent) retry loop in submitWithRetry() to cause a single slow node response to block all retries for the duration of the SDK's internal timeout, effectively reducing the number of gossip attempts within a given interval and increasing the probability of complete gossip failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera SDK to select the same failing node on every retry attempt (if the SDK's node selection is deterministic and not randomized), causing all cfg.maxRetries+1 attempts to fail against the same unreachable node and preventing gossip even when other healthy nodes are available?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() logs the transaction ID at line 38 (`resp.TransactionID.String()`) without sanitization, injecting control characters or newlines into the transaction ID field (if the SDK allows it) to corrupt log output and hide evidence of successful gossip from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the receipt status logged at line 37 to be misleading by exploiting a race between transaction submission and receipt polling, where the receipt is fetched before the transaction reaches consensus, causing a false UNKNOWN status to be logged and the transaction to be incorrectly treated as failed, triggering unnecessary retries?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the error returned by Execute(client) is not classified (transient vs. permanent) in submitWithRetry(), causing permanent errors (e.g., INVALID_SIGNATURE) to be retried cfg.maxRetries times unnecessarily, wasting operator funds and delaying the detection that gossip is permanently broken?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-gossip by exploiting the fact that submitWithRetry() does not implement circuit-breaking, so a permanently broken network configuration (e.g., all nodes unreachable) causes the pinger to retry indefinitely on every tick, consuming operator funds on failed submissions and never successfully gossiping any transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum amount validation beyond non-zero in cfg.amountTinybar to set it to 1 tinybar, causing the transfer to succeed but be economically meaningless, while the transaction fee (which may be orders of magnitude larger) drains the operator account, eventually causing INSUFFICIENT_PAYER_BALANCE and preventing future gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the context ctx passed to submitWithRetry() is the global application context (from main.go), so that any mechanism that triggers ctx cancellation (e.g., a SIGTERM signal sent to the process) immediately aborts all in-flight and pending transactions, preventing gossip of any transaction in the current retry window?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera SDK's Execute(client) to fail with a TLS handshake error by performing a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC connection to the consensus node (if TLS certificate pinning is not enforced), causing all attempts to fail and preventing gossip of any transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewTransferTransaction() does not set an explicit transaction memo or unique identifier beyond the SDK-generated transaction ID, causing the network to be unable to distinguish between legitimate pinger transactions and attacker-injected transactions with the same parameters, enabling the attacker to front-run or replay pinger transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera network to return PLATFORM_NOT_ACTIVE on every Execute(client) attempt by exploiting a known network maintenance window, causing all retries to fail and preventing gossip of any transaction during the maintenance period, with no alerting or escalation from the pinger?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `min(d, 30 * time.Second)` cap in backoff() to cause the pinger to sleep for exactly 30 seconds between retries when cfg.maxRetries is large, extending the total retry window to 30s * cfg.maxRetries seconds, which may exceed the Hedera transaction validity window (180s), causing the transaction to expire before all retries are exhausted and preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() does not record or persist the transaction ID of a successfully submitted (but unconfirmed) transaction, causing a crash or restart between Execute(client) and GetReceipt(client) to result in the transaction being gossiped but never confirmed, with no mechanism to detect or recover the orphaned transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger does not validate the receipt.Status field for specific success values (only checks rerr == nil), causing a transaction with an unexpected but non-error status to be treated as successful even if it was not properly gossiped or executed?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera SDK to fail Execute(client) with a gRPC RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED error by flooding the target consensus node with other transactions, causing the pinger's transaction to be rejected before gossip and all retries to fail against the same overloaded node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retry loop in submitWithRetry() does not implement node rotation between attempts, causing all retries to target the same consensus node, so that if that node is compromised or partitioned, all attempts fail and no transaction is gossiped to the broader network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the pinger to submit a transaction with an invalid signature by exploiting a key parsing vulnerability in the Hedera SDK (via a malformed cfg.operatorKey), causing Execute(client) to return INVALID_SIGNATURE on every attempt and preventing all transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the elapsed time logged at line 37 (`time.Since(start)`) includes both the Execute(client) and GetReceipt(client) durations, causing misleading latency metrics that mask the fact that Execute(client) succeeded (transaction gossiped) but GetReceipt(client) timed out, leading operators to incorrectly diagnose gossip failures?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger does not implement any rate limiting on its own transaction submissions, causing it to submit transactions at the maximum rate allowed by cfg.interval (e.g., 1 per second), which when combined with retry storms could cause the operator account to be throttled by the network and prevent gossip of legitimate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger does not verify that the destination account (toID) is a valid, active account before submitting the transaction, causing Execute(client) to succeed (transaction gossiped) but GetReceipt(client) to return INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID, causing the pinger to log a failure and retry, wasting operator funds?
